install.title: Install Orary
install.description: Add it to your home screen — works like a native app, no App Store needed

ios.safariOnly: Only works in Safari. If you're using Chrome or Firefox on iPhone, open this page in Safari first.
ios.step1Note: If you're already in Safari, you're good to go!
ios.step2: Tap the Share button at the bottom of Safari
ios.step3: Find "Add to Home Screen" in the share menu
ios.step3Warning: Scroll down! This option is NOT at the top of the share sheet — it's hidden below. Keep swiping down through the icons row until you see the "Add to Home Screen" icon with a plus symbol.
ios.step4Note: Orary will appear on your home screen — open it just like any app.

android.chromeOnly: Works best in Chrome. Samsung Browser also supports it.
android.step1: Tap the menu icon at the top-right of Chrome
android.step2: Select "Add to Home Screen" or "Install App"
android.step2Note: The label may vary depending on your Chrome version.
android.step3Note: Orary will appear on your home screen and in your app drawer.

pwa.title: Install Orary
pwa.subtitle: Add it to your home screen — works like a native app, no App Store needed

android.step2Title: Select "Add to Home Screen" or "Install App"

android.step3Desc: Orary will appear on your home screen and in your app drawer.

hero.phrase3: Because math shouldn't be this hard
hero.phrase5: Built by a worker, for workers

story.title: How it all started
story.p1: It all started in Australia. I was working there and noticed something that drove me crazy: every single week, my friends and I would sit down with a calculator, a notepad, and way too much patience trying to figure out how much we'd actually earn.
story.p2: Night hours? Different rate. Weekends? Another rate. Public holidays? Who even knows. And after all that math, the number on the payslip was never what we calculated. Every. Single. Time.
story.p3: So I thought: "Enough. There has to be a better way."
story.p4: I built spreadsheets. I tried templates. I made little tools that kind of worked... but never really nailed it. Nothing matched the actual payslip values. It was frustrating.
story.p5: Then one day it hit me:
story.p5highlight: "Why don't I build something real? Something at scale. Something that actually works AND helps me learn at the same time."
story.p6: And just like that, Orary was born. What started as a personal itch to scratch turned into a full-blown application that handles multiple rate types, automatic holiday detection, live tracking, bulk creation, exports, and way more than I ever imagined.
story.p7: Turns out, frustration is a pretty good fuel for building things.

creator.name: Marqui
creator.p1: I'm a developer from Argentina, currently based in New Zealand. I built Orary because I needed it myself, and I figured if it helped me, it could help others too.
creator.p2: I'm always working on different things, but I never stop pushing to grow in this world. I'll keep moving from place to place, and I hope the same happens with Orary — always evolving, always reaching new people.
creator.p3: If you have any questions, ideas, or just want to say hi, feel free to reach out.

claude.title: Powered by Claude
claude.subtitle: by Anthropic
claude.description: Some of the more complex features in Orary were built with the help of Claude AI, an assistant that made it possible to tackle challenges that would have taken much longer to solve alone.

insights.title: Your Work Pattern Analysis
insights.recommendation: Based on your recorded shifts, {{bestDay}} during the {{bestPeriod}} is your most profitable window — you average {{bestRate}}/h at your peak. With {{activeDays}} active day(s) of data, the pattern is clear: concentrating more hours during this window will have the greatest impact on your overall earnings. Keep tracking shifts to refine this analysis further.
insights.avoidNote: On the flip side, {{worstDay}} has been your least productive day so far — consider whether reallocating those hours could increase your total take-home pay.
insights.activeDays: Analysis based on {{count}} active day(s) · {{hours}}h of total recorded shifts
